AnchorType.description: What a summary (and a summary_refresh queue row) is about.
AnchorType.title: AnchorType

AssetVersionCreate.description:
Create a version. Either PROMOTE from an existing artifact (`from_artifact_id`,
zero-copy: the version copies the artifact's content_hash + full r2:// uri + size)
OR supply the pointer identity directly.
AssetVersionCreate.title: AssetVersionCreate

ClientRegistrationIn.description:
RFC 7591 dynamic client registration request. We accept the standard
metadata but fix grant/response types and the (none) auth method ourselves.
ClientRegistrationIn.title: ClientRegistrationIn

DeviceAuthorizationStart.description:
Start a first-party CLI browser handoff.

PKCE is mandatory even though the high-entropy device code is already a
bearer secret: it binds the exchange to the CLI process that initiated it
if a terminal transcript or launch URL is copied elsewhere.
DeviceAuthorizationStart.title: DeviceAuthorizationStart

ExperimentPatch.description:
Field-replace PATCH (D9). Only provided fields change; None = untouched.
`hypothesis`/`name` carry min_length=1 so a PATCH can never re-blank them.
ExperimentPatch.title: ExperimentPatch

ExperimentVersionMint.description:
Mint an immutable reproduction manifest (fold #6). `as_of` defaults to the
mint-time watermark; `exclude_run_ids` are recorded and left out of the refs.
ExperimentVersionMint.title: ExperimentVersionMint

GitHubInstallationClaim.description:
Post-install redirect claim: GitHub sends
?installation_id=...&state=...&code=... back to the Integrations page (Setup
URL), which POSTs all three here.

`state` is the signed token the install_url carried (team identity).
`code` is the user-authorization code GitHub adds when the App has
"Request user authorization (OAuth) during installation" enabled --
exchanged server-side to prove the claiming user ADMINISTERS the
installation (review F1: state alone cannot; installation ids are small
integers and the App-JWT lookup proves existence, never ownership).
Absent state is a POLICY rejection (422) except for the same-team
revival/re-claim path; absent code is a 422 when the server has the App's
OAuth client creds configured.
GitHubInstallationClaim.title: GitHubInstallationClaim

GitHubIntegrationState.description: Server-side integration state the Integrations page renders.
GitHubIntegrationState.title: GitHubIntegrationState

IndexCorpus.description:
The /v1/search corpus filter vocabulary (github.* and claude_code
transcript docs are engine-native, not custom-ingest projections).
IndexCorpus.title: IndexCorpus

LineageEntityType.description: The endpoints a lineage edge can connect (closed set).
LineageEntityType.title: LineageEntityType

MetricSeriesOut.description: One catalog row: what/how-much/what-shape, no point scan (D15).
MetricSeriesOut.title: MetricSeriesOut

PendingInviteOut.description: An invite shown to the invitee on /onboarding (joined to team name).
PendingInviteOut.title: PendingInviteOut

ProjectArtifactCreate.description: Project-anchored artifact (fold #22); same shape as the experiment-anchored one.
ProjectArtifactCreate.title: ProjectArtifactCreate

ProjectPatch.description:
Field-replace PATCH (D9). Only provided fields change; None = untouched.

`workspace_id` re-files the project into another workspace (D6: projects
stay re-fileable); it is resolved in-tenant before use (amendment #3).
ProjectPatch.title: ProjectPatch

RunBundle.description:
One request: run + catalog + artifact index (bounded) + spans overview +
lineage refs. Raw points are excluded (fetch via /series/query).
RunBundle.title: RunBundle

RunCounts.description: Child-row tallies nested under RunOut.counts (dashboard contract).
RunCounts.title: RunCounts

RunDetailOut.description:
The /v1 read view of a run: RunOut plus the fold fields that are DELIBERATELY
absent from RunOut so the frozen ingest response stays byte-identical (the ingest
route keeps response_model=RunOut, which filters these out). short_id (fold #21)
is the human-facing petname the dashboard displays; created_by (fold #1), env_ref
(fold #7), and foreign_keys (fold #8) surface here for reads.
RunDetailOut.title: RunDetailOut

RunGcRequest.description:
Permanently purge deleted runs (owner/admin). Exactly one selector: an
explicit id list, or every run soft-deleted before `older_than`.
RunGcRequest.title: RunGcRequest

RunGroupPatch.description: Field-replace PATCH (D9). Only provided fields change; None = untouched.
RunGroupPatch.title: RunGroupPatch

RunLineage.description: Ancestors + descendants of a run (recursive-CTE result).
RunLineage.title: RunLineage

Scope.description:
Coarse v1 authorization scopes (D2). Narrowable later; checked as explicit
sets per route, NOT as a hierarchy (a `write` token does not imply `read`).
Scope.title: Scope

ScopedUploadRequest.description:
Upload fields shared by project and experiment anchors.

The anchor comes exclusively from the URL and the R2 pointer/key are always
server-built. Forbid extras so a client-supplied ``uri`` or ``key`` is rejected
instead of being silently ignored by Pydantic.
ScopedUploadRequest.title: ScopedUploadRequest

SearchEntityType.description: Entities the exact SQL channel searches (names + slugs).
SearchEntityType.title: SearchEntityType

SemanticChunk.description:
One MATCHED chunk of a document, full content.

Not truncated to a fixed cap: when the response must shrink, the budget
drops whole chunks from the tail rather than clipping every chunk into
uselessness. `graph_evidence` is empty for a text-only match, so its
absence is itself signal.
SemanticChunk.title: SemanticChunk

SemanticRefKind.description: research-os entities a semantic doc id resolves back to.
SemanticRefKind.title: SemanticRefKind

SeriesQueryRequest.description: Multi-run, multi-series read for the run page AND N-run comparison (D9).
SeriesQueryRequest.title: SeriesQueryRequest

SpanUpsertResult.description: Typed replacement for the bare `{"upserted": n}` dict (D9).
SpanUpsertResult.title: SpanUpsertResult

SummaryState.description: Derived UI state for a summary (badge).
SummaryState.title: SummaryState

TeamCreate.description:
POST /auth/teams. `slug` format/uniqueness is validated
server-side (app.teams.service) so the 400/409 carries a helpful message +
a collision suggestion; the model only bounds length.
TeamCreate.title: TeamCreate

TeamMemberOut.description: One active team membership joined to its human identity.
TeamMemberOut.title: TeamMemberOut

TeamMemberRoleUpdate.title: TeamMemberRoleUpdate

TokenCreate.description:
POST /v1/tokens (session-only mint). `scopes=None` inherits the caller's
role; e.g. `['read']` mints a read-only 'notebook' token. `expires_at=None`
means non-expiring (house precedent).
TokenCreate.title: TokenCreate

TokenCreated.description:
Mint response — carries the plaintext secret ONCE (never stored, never
shown again).
TokenCreated.title: TokenCreated

TokenOut.description: A token as listed (never carries the secret).
TokenOut.title: TokenOut

WorkspaceKind.description:
The closed workspace kind vocabulary (mirrors the DB CHECK).

`SHARED` is RETIRED (decision 2026-07-16): app code never creates a shared
workspace anymore. It is kept here only so the API can still serialize a
legacy null-owner shared row on an existing install until it is retired by
`app.workspaces.retire_shared` and a future contract migration drops the kind.
WorkspaceKind.title: WorkspaceKind

WorkspaceOut.description: One dropdown entry: the dashboard's workspace switcher builds on this.
WorkspaceOut.title: WorkspaceOut

WorkspacePatch.description:
The one user-editable workspace field.

Slugs and ownership are stable server-managed identity. Names are display
labels, trimmed at the API boundary so whitespace-only values never reach
the database.
WorkspacePatch.title: WorkspacePatch

schema.title: Response Revoke Agent Tap Revoke Post

get.description: Verify the ID token, upsert the user, mint a session, 302 to dashboard.

post.description: Create a short-lived request. No user/session exists at this stage.

get.description: Consent metadata for the signed-in browser; never returns a secret.

post.description: Explicit session-only Allow/Deny with an atomic pending-state CAS.
Poll/exchange a decided request; mint at most one user-bound PAT.

get.description: Invites addressed to the signed-in user's verified email (onboarding).

schema.title: Response Pending Invites Auth Invites Pending Get

get.description: Begin the OIDC flow: stash the (validated) redirect, 302 to Google.

post.description: Revoke the session and clear the cookie (idempotent).

get.description: The signed-in user + their teams + the active team.

post.description:
Set the session's active team -- ONLY if the user is a member of it.

The body's customer_id is never trusted without the membership check; an
unowned team yields 403.

schema.title: Response Switch Team Auth Switch Team Post
Response List Invites Auth Teams Invites Get
Response List Members Auth Teams Members Get

delete.description: Remove a non-owner member and immediately clear their active team sessions.

patch.description: Promote/demote a non-owner member. Ownership transfer is not a v1 flow.

get.description:
The tap's remote killswitch poll. Static true for now; when a whole
customer needs transcript ingestion disabled, THIS is the seam that says
so (the tap stops sending instead of us 403ing every batch).

schema.title: Response Claude Code Ingest Status Ingest V1 Sessions Status Get

post.description:
Prune terminal 'failed' uploads older than `older_than` (owner/admin) -- the
manual second stage of the reaper's mark-then-prune. A 'failed' row is already
HEAD-verified absent + past the grace window, so deleting it is safe; a 'pending'
row can be a LIVE in-flight upload and is NEVER swept here (that was the pre-
auto-confirm behavior -- the reaper now transitions abandoned pending -> failed).
DB rows only -- R2 blob GC stays OFF.

delete.description:
Soft-delete the artifact row (idempotent-safe: an already-deleted or absent
artifact -> 404). The R2 blob is intentionally left in place (deferred gc).

get.description: Relay a bounded, allowlisted R2 blob as inert bytes for UI previews.

200.description: Bounded inert artifact bytes for an authenticated preview

parameters.description: Exact asset name. Without it, resolving an asset by name means walking every page of the registry, and a client that gives up part-way cannot tell 'not found' from 'stopped looking' -- which is how duplicate asset identities get created.
parameters.name: name

schema.description: Exact asset name. Without it, resolving an asset by name means walking every page of the registry, and a client that gives up part-way cannot tell 'not found' from 'stopped looking' -- which is how duplicate asset identities get created.
schema.title: Name
Response List Assets V1 Assets Get
Response List Versions V1 Assets  Asset Id  Versions Get

get.description: One query per level, never one per parent; `limit` applies PER level.

parameters.description: 'project:<uuid>' or 'experiment:<uuid>'. Omit for the top level.
parameters.name: scope

schema.description: 'project:<uuid>' or 'experiment:<uuid>'. Omit for the top level.
schema.title: Scope
Response List Devices V1 Devices Get
Response List Events V1 Events Get
Response List Experiments V1 Experiments Get

get.description:
``prefix`` scopes to one folder and everything under it (S3 prefix
semantics over the derived ``path``); omit it for the whole experiment.
``limit`` is opt-in — this endpoint has always been unbounded, and capping it
silently would truncate existing callers with no way for them to notice.

schema.title: Response List Experiment Artifacts V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Artifacts Get

get.description: Every edge touching a run or artifact that belongs to this experiment.

schema.title: Response List Experiment Edges V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Edges Get
Response List Groups V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Groups Get
Response List Versions V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Versions Get
Response Ingestion Stats V1 Integrations Ingestion Get
Response Retry Ingestion Dlq V1 Integrations Ingestion Retry Dlq Post
Response Get Backfill Status V1 Integrations  Source  Backfill Get

get.description:
The authenticated caller: identity + resolved tenant, role, and scopes.

`require_scopes()` with no required scope accepts any authenticated principal,
so this works for a session cookie, a `ros_pat`, or an OAuth access token.

parameters.description: only projects filed in this workspace
parameters.name: workspace_id

schema.description: only projects filed in this workspace
schema.title: Workspace Id
Response List Projects V1 Projects Get

get.description:
``prefix`` scopes to one folder and everything under it (S3 prefix
semantics over the derived ``path``); omit it for the whole project.
``limit`` is opt-in — this endpoint has always been unbounded, and capping it
silently would truncate existing callers with no way for them to notice.

schema.title: Response List Project Artifacts V1 Projects  Project Id  Artifacts Get

parameters.description: True (manual click): fire ASAP. False (client auto-refresh on visit): use the debounce window so a burst of visitors to an always-stale active project coalesces to one generation per staleness ceiling instead of one paid generation per visit.
parameters.name: immediate

schema.description: True (manual click): fire ASAP. False (client auto-refresh on visit): use the debounce window so a burst of visitors to an always-stale active project coalesces to one generation per staleness ceiling instead of one paid generation per visit.
schema.title: Immediate
Response List Runs V1 Runs Get

post.description:
Permanently purge soft-deleted runs (owner/admin). Selects by explicit id
list OR everything deleted before `older_than` (exactly one). Purges DB ROWS
ONLY — spans/metrics/artifacts CASCADE; R2 blobs are NOT touched (deferred).
Each purged run's children have their parent_run_id + parent_relation cleared
together first, so no dangling lineage marker is left behind.

delete.description:
Soft-delete: hide the run everywhere until restore or gc. The natural key
stays reserved. Deleting an already-deleted (or absent) run -> 404.

get.description:
List a run's artifacts, optionally filtered by kind, step range, or folder.

``kind`` matches the normalized (lowercased) column. Step bounds are
inclusive and compare against ``step_index``, so rows with no step are
excluded whenever either bound is set (SQL NULL comparison) — exactly what
step-scoped forensics ("sandbox state at steps 599..601") wants.

``prefix`` scopes to one folder and everything under it (S3 prefix semantics
over the derived ``path``): ``prefix=outputs`` returns ``outputs/loss.csv``
and ``outputs/plots/fig.png``, but not ``config.json``. Omit it for the whole
run. ``limit`` is opt-in and left unset by default: this endpoint has always
been unbounded, and capping it silently would truncate existing callers'
results with no way for them to notice.

schema.title: Response List Artifacts V1 Runs  Run Id  Artifacts Get
Response List Run Edges V1 Runs  Run Id  Edges Get
Response List Run Events V1 Runs  Run Id  Events Get

post.description:
Report that a run's process is still alive.

Liveness cannot be inferred from `status` alone: it is a plain column, so a
run whose process dies without a final PATCH stays 'running' forever. A
client calls this periodically (well inside `run_heartbeat_stale_seconds`)
and the reaper flips anything that stops beating to 'crashed'.

Beating is what makes a run REAPABLE -- rows that have never beat are left
alone forever. So this is safe to adopt gradually: a client that never calls
it keeps exactly the old behaviour.

Only a 'running' run is stamped. Beating a finished run would resurrect it
into the reaper's scan for no reason, and beating a soft-deleted one would
contradict the tombstone.

schema.title: Response List Metrics V1 Runs  Run Id  Metrics Get

post.description:
Un-delete a soft-deleted run. 404 if the run does not exist in-tenant;
a no-op (still 200) if it was already live.

schema.title: Response Get Series Catalog V1 Runs  Run Id  Series Get
Response List Spans V1 Runs  Run Id  Spans Get

post.description: Structure-only sugar: mints a step span. Numbers go through /metrics.

get.description: `run_ref` is a UUID or a petname short_id (fold #21); resolved unambiguously.

The run-page JSON dump (D9): run + catalog + bounded artifact index +
span-type overview + lineage refs. Raw points come from /series/query.
`run_ref` is a UUID or a petname short_id. 404 on a soft-deleted run;
child_run_ids + artifacts exclude deleted rows.

post.description:
One request serves the run page (1 run) and the comparison view (N runs).
RLS scopes the read; every run_id is validated in-tenant AND live (a soft-
deleted run is hidden -> 404) before any points are read.

get.description:
The team's Shared files: flat, name-ordered, live COMPLETE rows only
(names with slashes imply folders -- the same tree convention as the
workspace-file/artifact explorer). Team-wide: every member sees all of them.
A read never provisions the folder -- until the first share/upload the list
is simply empty.

``prefix`` scopes to one folder and everything under it (S3 prefix semantics
over the derived ``path``, 0029); ``limit`` is the opt-in sanity cap -- the
same folder-scoping its workspace-file sibling has, so both file surfaces
behave identically.

schema.title: Response List Shared Files V1 Shared Files Get

post.description:
Presign a direct upload INTO the team Shared folder (reuses the workspace-
file presign->confirm rails, anchored on shared_folder_id). The folder is
auto-provisioned on this first write.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a Shared file (idempotent-safe: absent/already-deleted/not-a-
shared-file -> 404). Tombstones the index doc in the same tx (D9). The R2
blob is left in place (deferred gc).

post.description:
Confirm a Shared-file upload (anchor-aware confirm; identical swap
semantics to a workspace file).

get.description:
Presigned GET for a Shared file (tenant-prefix verified, like the generic
artifact download). 404 unless the artifact is a live Shared-folder file.

post.description:
Reverse of share: move a Shared file back into the CALLER's personal
workspace (provisioned on first need). Symmetric re-anchor + index re-key +
name-collision handling: 409 by default, `replace=true` supersedes the prior
personal-workspace file atomically.

get.description: Flat single-GET (D9). Hidden when its run is soft-deleted.

schema.title: Response List Tokens V1 Tokens Get

delete.description:
CLI logout: the calling token revokes itself. 400 if the caller is a
session (there is no 'current token').

Revoke a token by id. Your own token: any writer. A teammate's token:
owner/admin (`admin` scope) AND a browser session (never a token).

post.description:
OWNERSHIP TRANSFER: move a personal-workspace file into the team Shared
folder. In ONE tenant tx: re-anchor (workspace_id -> shared_folder_id),
enforce the shared active-name uniqueness, and RE-KEY the search index
(tombstone the old `workspace:<id>` doc + upsert the new `shared:<customer>`
doc, SAME file:<id> doc id). Provenance is preserved.

Permissions posture (deferred-permissions): ANY WRITE-scope team member may
share ANY personal-workspace file -- ownership is not checked; only WRITE
scope + a currently workspace-anchored artifact. A run/experiment/project
artifact is NOT a file and cannot be shared (422).

Name collision in the Shared folder: 409 by default (never auto-supersede);
pass `replace=true` to atomically supersede the prior shared file (the same
confirm-time swap the artifacts rails already use).

schema.title: Response List Workspaces V1 Workspaces Get

patch.description:
Rename the caller's own personal workspace.

Tenant RLS is necessary but not sufficient here: every member can see the
team's filing locations, but a person's workspace label belongs to that
person. The owner predicate makes teammate, legacy-shared, foreign, and
missing targets the same 404.

get.description:
Flat, name-ordered live COMPLETE files; names with slashes imply folders
(the existing explorer contract builds the tree client-side). Pending rows
are staged replacements/first uploads (review F14) -- hidden here so a
mid-upload replacement never shadows or duplicates the live version.

``prefix`` scopes to one folder and everything under it (S3 prefix semantics
over the derived ``path``, 0029); omit it for the whole workspace. The flat,
name-ordered contract is unchanged -- prefix narrows the rows, it does not
reshape them into a tree.

schema.title: Response List Workspace Files V1 Workspaces  Workspace Id  Files Get

delete.description:
Soft-delete a file only while it is anchored to ``workspace_id``.

The workspace predicate is part of the UPDATE, not a client-side precheck:
PostgreSQL rechecks it after a concurrent ownership move. A stale explorer
row therefore returns 404 instead of deleting the file from Shared (or from
another workspace). The index tombstone and audit event share the tenant tx.
